message:
fix(security): deep audit URL injection fixes across Discord, Twitter, GitHub, Notion, Elasticsearch, Kubernetes, Stripe, S3

Systematic second-pass security audit found and fixed URL path/query
injection vulnerabilities across 8 more connectors:

- Discord: validate_snowflake_id() for all channel/guild/message/user IDs
- Twitter: validate_numeric_id() for all tweet/user/conversation IDs
- GitHub: validate_owner_repo() + encode_path() for file paths
- Notion: validate_notion_id() + encode block_id in query strings
- Elasticsearch: sanitize_path_segment() for index/document IDs
- Kubernetes: sanitize_path_segment() for namespaces/resources +
  encode_query_param() for label/field selectors
- Stripe: encode_path_segment() + encode_query_value() for IDs and params
- S3: encode_bucket() for bucket names in URLs + copy_source header

Total new tests added: ~100+. All connectors pass: Discord 218, Twitter 402,
GitHub 239, Notion 218, Elasticsearch 204, Kubernetes 508, Stripe 227, S3 200.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,6 +26,7 @@ fcp-sdk = { path = "../../crates/fcp-sdk" }
 
 anyhow.workspace = true

 reqwest.workspace = true
